nodes.label: Feature Freeze (Phase 0)
nodes.rationale: In effect until the W5 validation round passes or fails: no fixes, polish, or refactors on frozen surfaces, crash fixes only. Every hour on tutor-vision surfaces is an hour not validating the wedge. Shared infrastructure the active Discover→Study→Correct loop depends on is explicitly NOT frozen.

nodes.label: PhraseLoop
nodes.rationale: Helps Brazilian A2-B1 self-study learners turn real English and their mistakes into native-audio review cards on Mac. One Home-led loop first; speech generation, Anki export, Speak, plans, and provider setup stay behind the core loop.

nodes.label: Five-Second Promise
nodes.rationale: Turn real English and your mistakes into native-audio review cards in 2 minutes. The switching bet is speed: the path from source material to a reviewed card must be under 2 minutes with less friction than Anki, Migaku, or LingQ.

nodes.label: Native Source Audio Differentiator
nodes.rationale: Phrases come from real English the user chose and review cards keep that native audio, not a robotic re-read — something manual flashcard apps and generic chatbots don't do.

nodes.label: Mistakes Become Drills Differentiator
nodes.rationale: A correction isn't a dead-end note: each fix turns into a review card, and weak spots feed back into more practice.

nodes.label: Local Speech Runtime (Kokoro + Whisper)
nodes.rationale: Kokoro 1.0 via sherpa-onnx and whisper.cpp/Metal loaded directly by Node — no Python, no helper app, no auxiliary Dock icon. Models downloaded on first use, verified by SHA-256, installed atomically.

nodes.label: YouTube Audio Import Pipeline (yt-dlp + whisper.cpp)
nodes.rationale: Downloads audio only (no video) with yt-dlp because YouTube.js can no longer decipher stream URLs; transcribes locally with whisper.cpp producing a timestamped transcript. Transcription runs 100% locally.

nodes.label: Pluggable AI Providers
nodes.rationale: Ollama is the default; PhraseLoop never sends content to a cloud provider unless explicitly selected. OpenRouter, Claude, and GPT are opt-in via keys. First lesson and first review show no provider setup.

nodes.label: Closed Loop Thesis (capturar → estudar → produzir → reforçar)
nodes.rationale: Language tools optimize passive consumption or isolated memorization; the annoying work lives in between — the step before Anki. PhraseLoop is not trying to beat Anki as a repetition engine but to win the step before it: find, extract, clean, add audio, review, and reuse mistakes.

nodes.label: ICP: A2–B1 Brazilian Self-Study / Anki-Adjacent Learner
nodes.rationale: Deliberately narrow v1 cut: Brazilian PT speaker learning English, A2-B1, already self-studies with real content, tried Anki-like flows, owns an Apple Silicon Mac. Consequences: PT-BR primary interface, first value within ~2 minutes, and no AI provider requirement for the first session.

nodes.label: Pedagogical Method: Governed Adaptive Cycles
nodes.rationale: Not 'always push the learner out of the comfort zone' but governing cycles between challenge, consolidation, recovery, and return. FSRS and active recall at the center; latency/hint/fatigue capture to detect overload; light session/cooldown for bad days; adaptive difficulty behind a gate; no XP, leagues, or empty gamification.

nodes.label: Guided First-Run Loop
nodes.rationale: A single visible track in the first session proving both differentiators without explanation: hear curated clip → save phrase → review with native audio → write a sentence → get corrected → correction becomes tomorrow's card. Own import is never the first proof — it can fail, take long, or demand concepts before activation.

nodes.label: Sources as Source of Truth (Cards are Derivation)
nodes.rationale: ErrorEvents and PhraseCandidates persist, not just derived cards. The raw history of what you got wrong and what you chose to learn is what makes the tutor (weakness detection) possible. Nothing leaves the device unless the user explicitly picks a cloud provider.

nodes.label: Business Model Hypothesis (Free Local + One Paid Pain)
nodes.rationale: Status: hypothesis, no billing in v1. Free local-first tier (Kokoro/Whisper, bundled lessons, local SRS, BYO-key generation) plus one candidate paid pain (managed cloud generation, review-anywhere sync, or curated graded content). Cut rule: if fewer than 3/10 ICP users name the same paid pain concretely, billing stays off the roadmap.

nodes.label: Qualified Waitlist (W5 Demand Test)
nodes.rationale: PT-BR landing with required qualifying questions (platform, workflow) feeding the Phase 2 demand test; reuses the desktop app's UI via the @/* alias so it must be built inside the monorepo. The platform question feeds the W5 decision gate.

nodes.label: POST /api/waitlist Endpoint
nodes.rationale: Validates email format, platform enum, workflow answer ≥ 8 chars server-side, then forwards JSON to PHRASELOOP_WAITLIST_WEBHOOK_URL. Without the env var entries are only logged and lost on deployment recycle — do not run the demand test without the webhook.

nodes.label: Card Pipeline (Two Ingestion Paths, One Output)
nodes.rationale: Discovery (mine phrases from native content) and Correction (turn own mistakes into cards) converge on the same Card and the same .apkg export.

nodes.label: Discovery Ingestion (Extract → Curate → Review)
nodes.rationale: Three distinct steps so the user gets both automation and control: mechanical extraction (Whisper → TranscriptSegment[]), LLM curation (provider.mine biased by an optional focus), and human-in-the-loop review — only accepted candidates flow into provider.generate().

nodes.label: Correction Ingestion (ErrorEvent Path)
nodes.rationale: The Correct tab builds ErrorEvents (typed original→corrected or JSON paste); /api/cards/generate maps them to CardSource{kind:'error'} and runs the same generateDeck, so mistakes flow into the same store, .apkg engine, FSRS scheduling, and weakness analysis as discoveries.

nodes.label: Native Clip Slicing
nodes.rationale: With Whisper timestamps the exact native audio clip for each phrase is cut and embedded in the card — authentic intonation and rhythm TTS can't deliver, something no competitor does. Text sources without timestamps fall back to Kokoro TTS.

nodes.label: Provider-Agnostic Generation Contract
nodes.rationale: Everything past generate() is provider-agnostic; the pipeline never branches on provider. All providers share one brain (shared.ts prompt/schema builders + normalizers) so they stay genuinely interchangeable, and 'run it all locally' stays a coherent private path end to end. Orchestration: generateVettedCards and generateDeck in src/lib/cards/provider.ts.

nodes.label: Critique Gate (keep / rewrite / drop)
nodes.rationale: Quality gate after generation; rewrites inherit the original card's grounding. With Ollama (the local default) the critique step is skipped — slow local LLMs don't justify the cost — leaving grounding and the per-source card cap.

nodes.label: Card Grounding
nodes.rationale: Anti-hallucination: structural (source pointer set by code, not the model, verified in generateVettedCards) plus semantic (critique drops ungrounded cards). Every card points to a real source the learner has.

nodes.label: Semantic Dedup (Embedding Cosine)
nodes.rationale: src/lib/cards/dedupe.ts — embedding cosine when the provider has an embedder (OpenAI), lexical-cosine fallback otherwise.

nodes.label: SRS Engine (ts-fsrs)
nodes.rationale: ts-fsrs wrapped in src/lib/srs/fsrs.ts with Date↔epoch-ms serialization so the due index is range-queryable. New cards start due immediately; Study shows due queue, flip-to-answer, Again/Hard/Good/Easy with live next-interval preview.

nodes.label: Weakness Detection (The Tutor)
nodes.rationale: detectWeaknesses groups the review log by concept, error type, and context, ranks by struggle rate past a min-reviews threshold; denormalized fields mean it survives card deletion. Weaknesses carry improving/worsening/stable trends read from ErrorEvent production halves.

nodes.label: Reinforcement Loop (Reforçar)
nodes.rationale: Each weak spot gets a Reforçar button pulling every card for that concept/error-type into a focused drill via getReinforcementCards, bypassing the FSRS due date — turning the weakness panel from a passive report into an action: detect → drill.

nodes.label: Directed Generation (Gerar +)
nodes.rationale: getReinforcementSources finds the PhraseCandidates/ErrorEvents behind a weakness's struggled cards; POST /api/cards/reinforce runs them back through the same generateDeck to mint fresh variant cards drilling the same concept from a new angle — grounded by construction, no new material needed, no .apkg built (variants studied in-app).

nodes.label: Conversation Practice (Converse)
nodes.rationale: Not a disconnected chatbot but another ingestion path: conversation → mistakes → cards → SRS → weaknesses → reinforcement, with the scenario as situational context. Correction runs at end of session, not per turn; push-to-talk v1; cloud usage clearly disclosed.

nodes.label: Structured 90-Day Learning Plan
nodes.rationale: LLM-generated plan from LearningProfile, split into thematic phases with concrete DailyTasks mapping to existing features; adapts automatically by comparing plan vs. actual measured effort every 7 days.

nodes.label: Activity Log
nodes.rationale: IndexedDB store recording what the user really did (cards_reviewed, video_processed, conversation_turn, correction_generated, cards_created); each feature emits events and the plan engine reads them to mark tasks done — required because each feature otherwise lives in its own state.

nodes.label: EffortSnapshot (Weekly Effort Measurement)
nodes.rationale: Weekly plannedMinutes vs actualMinutes, adherenceRate, and streak derived automatically from the activity log — no manual logging. Drives plan adaptation rules (reduce load, advance phase, recovery task).

nodes.label: C1 Post-B1/B2 Plateau Phase Proposal
nodes.rationale: Concept-stage, gated on a W5 pass (Phase 6). Grammar-error correction stops being the binding constraint at C1; naturalness, register, idiomaticity, and collocation become the gap — an acknowledged critique, not a validated finding. No curated content ladder (that's a content-ops business), one domain only, MVP is diagnosis + one write→feedback→speak loop.

nodes.label: C1 Diagnosis Instrument
nodes.rationale: The reviewed proposal's biggest flaw was a gap with no defined measurement — a dashboard around a black box. Grammar gaps reuse computePerformance errorTypes (already exists); register/naturalness gaps get an LLM-graded writing sample scored against a rubric grown from CEFR_LANGUAGE_PROFILE / cefrLanguageLine(). Trust rule: every gap label must show the evidence next to it — never a bare skill tag.

nodes.label: Write → Feedback → Speak Pipeline
nodes.rationale: The protected differentiator: the writing→speaking transfer chain (read → collect vocab → write → get feedback → speak) is the one piece not already offered by LingQ or Migaku. Kill signal: learners accept Weak labels but never act on them — a report, not a habit.

nodes.label: Intercom-Inspired Design Language
nodes.rationale: Warm cream canvas (#faf9f6), off-black text (#111111), Fin Orange (#ff5600) as the singular brand accent, oat borders (#dedbd6), sharp 4px button radius, scale(1.1) hover, minimal shadows — depth from warm border tones and surface tints, never cool grays.

nodes.label: Product One-Sentence (Fonte de Verdade)
nodes.rationale: The canonical sentence — Brazilian A2-B1 self-study learners turning real English and their own mistakes into daily native-audio review on Mac — is the source of truth for README, onboarding, landing copy, release notes, and roadmap.

nodes.label: W5 Decision Record
nodes.rationale: Machine-written block (yarn w5:score) recording all 7 gate outcomes, primary route, billing route, and launch segment. Currently pending with 0/10 rows scored; the roadmap is subordinate to this evidence before launch.

nodes.label: Central Identity Decision (Local-First Tutor, Depth Hidden)
nodes.rationale: PhraseLoop looked like both a consumer app and a power-user tool: strong value sat behind AI config, Ollama, API keys, and Anki export while the UX promise pointed at a beginner. v1 decision: local-first PT→EN tutor, beginner path works with zero setup, advanced tools exist as depth, not as the front door.

nodes.label: Competitive Positioning (The Step Before Anki)
nodes.rationale: Not trying to beat Anki as a repetition engine — winning the step before it: find, extract, clean, add audio, review, reuse errors. Measurable promise: real clip/article to a reviewed native-audio card in under 2 minutes with less friction than Anki/Migaku/LingQ. Speak/ELSA are stronger on pronunciation.

nodes.label: Applied Research Principles (Flow, ZDP, Challenge Point, Desirable Difficulties)
nodes.rationale: Flow (challenge/skill balance), ZDP (slightly beyond solo ability with temporary scaffold), Challenge Point (functional difficulty depends on user and moment), desirable difficulties, performance ≠ learning, spacing/retrieval as the spine, selective interleaving. Translation: FSRS + active recall central, latency/hint/fatigue capture, opt-in removable scaffold, cooldown sessions, gated adaptive difficulty, no XP/leagues/aggressive notifications.

nodes.label: Adaptive Cycle Shipped Infrastructure
nodes.rationale: Review telemetry (ReviewRecord latency/hint/scaffold), honest end screen (SessionSummary separates accuracy now vs predicted stability), Study scaffold, light session/cooldown (sessionMode.ts), per-skill state (skillState.ts), cycle picker (cyclePlanner.ts), gated difficulty band (band.ts/bandQueue.ts only reorders when the gate says adopt). D+1/D+7 validation still pending.

nodes.label: Activation Event Model (product.md)
nodes.rationale: Minimum events first_run_started through day_7_returned; derived metrics activation, ttfr, and dropoff_step (clip | save_phrase | review | mistake | correction | own_source).

nodes.label: App Boundaries and Runtime Shape
nodes.rationale: Root app is the local-first desktop product (Electron → standalone Next → api routes → server/native); apps/landing is the only Vercel-deployed app. No Nest service exists — do not add one until Next API routes can't handle a hosted-backend need cleanly. Keeps the repo organized without a risky rewrite while W5 is active.

nodes.label: Source Directory Rules
nodes.rationale: app/api is a thin HTTP boundary; features hold user-facing product areas; lib holds pure shared domain logic; server holds server-only native work. Keep the visible app focused on Home → Learn → Discover → Study → Correct until W5 decides the next direction.

nodes.label: Validation Action Plan (Phases 0–6)
nodes.rationale: The vision is an LLM personal English teacher; the wedge (real English + own mistakes → native-audio cards for PT-BR A2-B1 self-learners) is the road there because the tutor is only defensible on top of longitudinal learner memory. Phases: 0 freeze, 1 W5 blockers, 2 run W5 + demand test, 3 decision gate, 4 trust & proof, 5 marketable, 6 tutor vision unlock.

nodes.label: Native Clips W5 Blocker
nodes.rationale: Replace first-run TTS audio with real native clips: the #1 differentiator is 'native audio, not a robotic re-read' — it must be true in minute one or the W5 differentiation gate measures a lie. Pipeline shipped 2026-07-05; the 36 recordings themselves are the outstanding step; yarn learn:audio:verify machine-checks the gate.

nodes.label: Own-Source Funnel
nodes.rationale: first_loop_completed → own_source_started → own_source_completed is the single most important conversion: the guided loop proves the mechanic, the own-source moment proves the value. Warning sign: <50% attempt their own source means the wedge story is wrong.

nodes.label: W5 Decision-Gate Scorer (yarn w5:score)
nodes.rationale: scripts/score-w5-decision-gate.mjs parses the capture table, scores all seven gates literally, routes the outcome, keeps billing a separate freeze decision, picks a launch segment only on a clear measured winner (≥3 rows, ≥5 signal points, ≥2-point lead), accepts waitlist platform mix JSON, and rewrites the W5 Decision Record in product.md. Do not let friendly calls override what the scorer says.

nodes.label: Phase 6 Tutor Vision Unlock
nodes.rationale: What a W5 pass + retention unlocks, in order: outcome measurement first, level progression (wire estimatedBand()/nextLevel() into a calm promotion prompt), onboarding depth, first-loop correction quality, unfreezing tutor surfaces one at a time behind D+30 evidence, pronunciation research, A1/C1-C2 band mechanisms, then multi-language.

nodes.label: Landing Demand Test
nodes.rationale: W5 proves activation among hand-picked Mac owners; only the landing + community posts test whether a reachable audience exists behind the compounded filter (Brazilian × A2-B1 × Anki-adjacent × Apple Silicon × desktop). Green: ≥30% visitor→signup and ≥25% Apple Silicon; red: Windows/mobile skew forces a platform strategy change.

nodes.label: W5 Validation Protocol
nodes.rationale: System of record for the W5 first-run round: validate whether a fresh ICP user completes the core loop, understands why it's useful, and shows a real return signal without coaching. 5-session smoke test, 10-session ICP decision round; clean installs must clear localStorage and IndexedDB or the W5 readout desyncs.

nodes.label: W5 Gate Metrics (7 Gates)
nodes.rationale: Unaided completion ≥6/10, median TTFR <2min, explain-back ≥7/10, D+1 ≥40% / D+7 ≥25%, differentiation ≥3/10 unprompted, paid pain ≥3/10 same answer, replacement ≥3/10. Judge the round, not one noisy session; failure modes route to front-door fixes, positioning work, billing freeze, or research-build status.

nodes.label: Hear → Review → Fix Loop
nodes.rationale: The loop under test: curated native clip → save one phrase → review → write one sentence → correct → save correction. Never explained before the first run — the interface must do the work. The guided lesson corrects locally with no AI provider so the loop completes on a clean install.

nodes.label: W5 Event Model
nodes.rationale: Activation events first_run_started..day_7_returned with derived ttfr and dropoff_step; app telemetry may derive some from lower-level activity events (cards_created, cards_reviewed, correction_generated) but W5 notes use the canonical names.

nodes.label: Backup/Restore Moderated Validation Protocol
nodes.rationale: One participant with 2+ weeks of organic history performs export → wipe → restore, moderated, proving zero loss per store (11 stores) including surviving FSRS due dates. Restore merges by ID and never deletes; the imported-audio cache on disk is not in the backup so restored cards fall back to TTS until re-import.

nodes.label: W5 Capture Table
nodes.rationale: One row per participant; smoke-test and decision-round results are never averaged together. Explain-back passes only when unprompted; paid pain must use exactly managed-cloud, review-anywhere, curated-content, none, or other:short-label because Phase 3 needs the same concrete answer from ≥3/10 users.

nodes.label: 90-Second Demo Video Script & Shot List
nodes.rationale: Phase 4 trust artifact serving both the landing page and community posts. Everything on screen must be real — real loop, real native audio, real own-source import; system audio ON because the native clip is the differentiator and must be heard, not described; never trim the payoff (the error card on day 2).

nodes.label: Demo Honesty Gate
nodes.rationale: Shot 2 ('não voz de robô') cannot be recorded over Kokoro audio — blocked until the Phase 1 real-native-clips item ships, or the claim is recorded over an own-source import (real audio by construction), or the video is held.

nodes.label: D+1/D+7 Follow-Up Messages
nodes.rationale: Exact PT-BR copy for the retention pings; activity-log returns are logged separately from self-report (Y vs Y (self-reported)) and the sources are never blended.

nodes.label: W5 Moderator Run-Sheet
nodes.rationale: Session choreography: clean install, consent, stopwatch at handover, silence until the participant completes the loop or gives up, timestamped observation of every loop step, then the ordered explain-back/differentiation/replacement/uninstall/paid-pain questions.

nodes.label: W5 Recruiting Message
nodes.rationale: PT-BR DM/community post targeting Brazilian A2-B1 self-study learners on Apple Silicon; screening prioritizes self-study/Anki users, one bundled-only beginner, one participant bringing their own source; never schedules Windows/mobile-only users or anyone unavailable for D+1/D+7.

nodes.label: Native Audio Clip Library (native-audio/)
nodes.rationale: Committed source of truth for genuine native-speaker clips; recordings mirror clip paths in lessons.json; yarn learn:audio installs them into public/ and never synthesizes over a native recording, --force included.

nodes.label: Provenance Manifest (manifest.json)
nodes.rationale: Every clip requires a provenance entry with mandatory speaker and license fields — the build fails without it. Licensed third-party audio must record the exact license and origin.

nodes.label: First-Run Audio Gate (yarn learn:audio:verify)
nodes.rationale: Machine-checkable half of the Phase 1 blocker: exits 1 until every clip of the guided first-run lessons (a1-greetings, a2-food, b1-opinions, plus 12 /demo/audio clips = 36 total) is a native recording. Record in priority order a2-food → b1-opinions → /demo/audio → a1-greetings (ICP first).

hyperedges.label: Card Pipeline Quality Stages (mine → generate → ground → critique → dedup)
Tutor Loop (SRS → weakness detection → reinforcement → directed generation)
